response:
To find the number of calories remaining in the box, we first calculate the total number of calories in the box. Each bar has 90 calories, and there are 12 bars per box, so the total calories in the box are:

$$
12 \text{ bars} \times 90 \text{ calories/bar} = 1080 \text{ calories}
$$

Maria has already eaten 3 bars, so the number of calories she has eaten is:

$$
3 \text{ bars} \times 90 \text{ calories/bar} = 270 \text{ calories}
$$

To find the remaining calories, we subtract the calories she has eaten from the total calories in the box:

$$
1080 \text{ calories} - 270 \text{ calories} = 810 \text{ calories}
$$

The remaining calories in the box are 810.

Calories remaining in the box: 810

Calories remaining in the box: 810
